qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncaught exceptions in ONNX parser numeric conversion functions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns numeric literal parsing in onnx/defs/parser.h and uncaught std::sto* exceptions, while the advisory concerns external data loading in onnx/checker.cc where symlinks are accepted as regular files.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow via recursive GraphProto nesting without depth limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded recursive GraphProto validation and parsing around checker.cc recursive check_graph calls, while the advisory concerns symlink traversal in external data file checks around checker.cc is_regular_file handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a symlink path traversal in ONNX external data loading caused by treating a symlink target as a regular file, enabling arbitrary host file reads. The qualifying findings instead cover numeric parser exceptions and recursive GraphProto denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-3r9x-f23j-gc73 describes arbitrary file read via symlinked external data and ineffective is_regular_file checks in onnx/checker.cc.
vuln_parser_exception_dos reports std::stoll, std::stoull, std::stof, and std::stod exception handling issues in onnx/defs/parser.h.
vuln_recursive_graph_dos reports recursive GraphProto nesting and missing recursion depth limits in onnx/proto_utils.h and onnx/checker.cc check_graph recursion.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding is present, so runtime tiering and PoC execution are not applicable.
